title: School/Office Supplies
description: Learn school/office supplies in Filipino Sign Language

pages.title: Welcome to School/Office Supplies
pages.content: In this lesson, you'll learn how to sign school/office supplies in Filipino Sign Language (FSL). You'll learn 12 important signs with step-by-step instructions and practice questions.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Pencils'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Pencils'. An implement for writing or drawing with graphite. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'P' handshape (index and middle fingers extended). 2. Do a writting gesture in air

pages.title: How to Sign 'Pens'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Pens'. An implement for writing or drawing with ink. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand to do the handwritting gesture

pages.title: How to Sign 'Notebooks'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Notebooks'. A book of blank paper for writing notes. 

How to sign:
1. Make the letter 'N' Handshape
2. Simulate the opening of a notebook using the 'N' Handshape

pages.title: How to Sign 'Folders'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Folders'. A folding cover used to hold loose papers. 

How to sign:
1. Make the letter 'F' Handshape
2. Simulate the opening of a folder using the 'F' Handshape

pages.title: How to Sign 'Chalk'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Chalk'. A soft, porous limestone used for writing or drawing. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant 'X' handshape (as a piece of chalk). 
2. Do the writting gesture in air

pages.title: How to Sign 'Erasers'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Erasers'. A piece of soft rubber or plastic used to remove marks. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant 'E' handshape. 
2. Move/Rub the dominant hand across the non-dominant flat palm, simulating erasing.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Rulers'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Rulers'. A straight strip of material used for drawing straight lines or measuring. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant 'R' handshape 
2. Touch back forearm of the non-dominant hand.
3. Slide the dominant 'R' hadshape to simulate measuring.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Glue sticks'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Glue sticks'. A solid adhesive in a twist-up tube. 

How to sign:
1. Use '3' and '5' finger and create a pinching gesture on both hands to simulate glue/sticky
2. On both hands use '4' and '5' finger and create a visulization of a stick.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Scissors'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Scissors'. A cutting instrument consisting of two pivoted blades. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'V' handshape, palm facing out. 2. Open and close the fingers like scissors cutting in the air.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Backpack'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Backpack'. A bag with shoulder straps, carried on the back. 

How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand to touch your shoulder
2. Make a curved using the non-dominant hand from the dominant hand to elbow to  create a letter 'B'
3. Do the wearing backpack gesture.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Lunch box'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Lunch box'. A container used to hold a packed lunch. 

How to sign:
1. Sign LUNCH: Dominant 'L' hand on the chin. 2. Sign BOX: Two 'B' hands form a square.

pages.title: How to Sign 'Water bottle'
pages.content:
Learn how to sign 'Water bottle'. A container used to hold water for drinking. 

How to sign:
1. Sign WATER ('W' hand on chin). 2. Sign BOTTLE: Dominant 'C' hand moves up from the non-dominant palm.

pages.title: Practice: Lunch box
pages.content: Test your understanding with this question.

question.text: What is the sign for 'Lunch box'?

question.explanation:
This sign means 'Lunch box'. A container used to hold a packed lunch.

How to sign: 1. Sign LUNCH: Dominant 'L' hand on the chin. 2. Sign BOX: Two 'B' hands form a square.
